mnemonics: DAD'S CAR: Data structures Are Designed for Speedy CRUD (Create, Read, Update, Delete) operations
keyPoints: Understand the time and space complexity (Big O notation) of common operations for each data structure.
Know the key differences between similar structures (e.g., Array vs. ArrayList, LinkedList vs. ArrayList, Stack vs. Queue).
Be able to implement the core data structures (e.g., linked list, stack, queue, binary tree) and their fundamental operations from pseudocode or scratch.
commonMistakes: Confusing the properties of different structures (e.g., assuming a LinkedList has O(1) access time for any element like an array).
Incorrectly calculating or stating the time complexity for an operation, especially for operations within a loop (e.g., not recognizing an O(n) operation inside an O(n) loop is O(n²)).
